title: Mount Kaputar National Park

infobox.value: * Regional Board, [Australia](/wiki/Australia "Australia") * [National Parks and Wildlife Service](/wiki/National_Parks_and_Wildlife_Service_(New_South_Wales) "National Parks and Wildlife Service (New South Wales)")

* [Abercrombie](/wiki/Abercrombie_River_National_Park "Abercrombie River National Park") * [Breelong](/w/index.php?title=Breelong_National_Park&action=edit&redlink=1 "Breelong National Park (page does not exist)") * [Capertee](/wiki/Capertee_National_Park "Capertee National Park") * [Conimbla](/wiki/Conimbla_National_Park "Conimbla National Park") * [Coolah Tops](/wiki/Coolah_Tops_National_Park "Coolah Tops National Park") * [Drillwarrina](/w/index.php?title=Drillwarrina_National_Park&action=edit&redlink=1 "Drillwarrina National Park (page does not exist)") * [Gardens of Stone](/wiki/Gardens_of_Stone_National_Park "Gardens of Stone National Park") * [Garrawilla](/wiki/Garrawilla_National_Park "Garrawilla National Park") * [Goobang](/wiki/Goobang_National_Park "Goobang National Park") * [Goulburn](/wiki/Goulburn_River_National_Park "Goulburn River National Park") * [Kanangra-Boyd](/wiki/Kanangra-Boyd_National_Park "Kanangra-Boyd National Park") * [Lachlan Valley](/w/index.php?title=Lachlan_Valley_National_Park&action=edit&redlink=1 "Lachlan Valley National Park (page does not exist)") * [Marrangaroo](/w/index.php?title=Marrangaroo_National_Park&action=edit&redlink=1 "Marrangaroo National Park (page does not exist)") * [Nangar](/wiki/Nangar_National_Park "Nangar National Park") * [Popran](/wiki/Popran_National_Park "Popran National Park") * [Turon](/wiki/Turon_National_Park "Turon National Park") * [Warrumbungle](/wiki/Warrumbungle_National_Park "Warrumbungle National Park") * [Weddin Mountains](/wiki/Weddin_Mountains_National_Park "Weddin Mountains National Park") * [Wollemi](/wiki/Wollemi_National_Park "Wollemi National Park")

is a [national park](/wiki/National_park "National park") located in [New South Wales](/wiki/New_South_Wales "New South Wales"), [Australia](/wiki/Australia "Australia"), surrounding the proximities of [Mount Kaputar](/wiki/Mount_Kaputar "Mount Kaputar"), a volcano active between 17 and 21 million years ago.[[3]](#cite_note-park-id-3) It is located 50 km (31 mi) east of [Narrabri](/wiki/Narrabri "Narrabri") and 570 km (354 mi) northwest of [Sydney](/wiki/Sydney "Sydney").[[4]](#cite_note-tourism-info-4) Millions of years of [erosion](/wiki/Erosion "Erosion") have since carved the volcanic region into the [lava terraces](/wiki/Lava "Lava"), [volcanic plugs](/wiki/Volcanic_plug "Volcanic plug"), and [dykes](/wiki/Dike_(geology) "Dike (geology)") of [Nandewar Range](/wiki/Nandewar_Range "Nandewar Range"). The central feature of the region is Mount Kaputar, the park's namesake, which rises to an altitude of 1,510 m (4,954 ft).[[3]](#cite_note-park-id-3) The 360 degree view from the summit of the mountain encompasses one-tenth of New South Wales' area or 80,000 square kilometres (31,000 sq mi).[[4]](#cite_note-tourism-info-4)

["Mount Kaputar National Park Plan of Management"](https://www.environment.nsw.gov.au/-/media/OEH/Corporate-Site/Documents/Parks-reserves-and-protected-areas/Parks-plans-of-management/mount-kaputar-national-park-plan-of-management-210368.pdf) (PDF). Retrieved 31 January 2023.

From Wikipedia, the free encyclopedia

National park in New South Wales, Australia

| Mount Kaputar National Park | |

| Location | [New South Wales](/wiki/New_South_Wales "New South Wales") |
| Coordinates | [30°17′26″S 150°08′34″E﻿ / ﻿30.29056°S 150.14278°E﻿ / -30.29056; 150.14278](https://geohack.toolforge.org/geohack.php?pagename=Mount_Kaputar_National_Park&params=30_17_26_S_150_08_34_E_type:landmark_dim:23km) |
| Area | 502.25 km2 (193.92 sq mi)[[1]](#cite_note-pom-1) |
| Governing body | * Regional Board, [Australia](/wiki/Australia "Australia") * [National Parks and Wildlife Service](/wiki/National_Parks_and_Wildlife_Service_(New_South_Wales) "National Parks and Wildlife Service (New South Wales)") |
| Managing Authorities[[2]](#cite_note-park-hist-2) | |

The **Mount Kaputar National Park** is a [national park](/wiki/National_park "National park") located in [New South Wales](/wiki/New_South_Wales "New South Wales"), [Australia](/wiki/Australia "Australia"), surrounding the proximities of [Mount Kaputar](/wiki/Mount_Kaputar "Mount Kaputar"), a volcano active between 17 and 21 million years ago.[[3]](#cite_note-park-id-3) It is located 50 km (31 mi) east of [Narrabri](/wiki/Narrabri "Narrabri") and 570 km (354 mi) northwest of [Sydney](/wiki/Sydney "Sydney").[[4]](#cite_note-tourism-info-4) Millions of years of [erosion](/wiki/Erosion "Erosion") have since carved the volcanic region into the [lava terraces](/wiki/Lava "Lava"), [volcanic plugs](/wiki/Volcanic_plug "Volcanic plug"), and [dykes](/wiki/Dike_(geology) "Dike (geology)") of [Nandewar Range](/wiki/Nandewar_Range "Nandewar Range"). The central feature of the region is Mount Kaputar, the park's namesake, which rises to an altitude of 1,510 m (4,954 ft).[[3]](#cite_note-park-id-3) The 360 degree view from the summit of the mountain encompasses one-tenth of New South Wales' area or 80,000 square kilometres (31,000 sq mi).[[4]](#cite_note-tourism-info-4)

The park protects a wide range of [biomes](/wiki/Biome "Biome"), including [semi-arid woodland](/wiki/Woodland "Woodland"), [subalpine](/wiki/Subalpine "Subalpine") [heath](/wiki/Heath_(habitat) "Heath (habitat)"), and [eucalypt forests](/wiki/Eucalypt "Eucalypt"), and provides a habitat for a range of animals, including [bats](/wiki/Bat "Bat"), [birds](/wiki/Bird "Bird"), [wallabies](/wiki/Wallaby "Wallaby"), [quolls](/wiki/Quoll "Quoll"), and the unique [red triangle slug](/wiki/Red_triangle_slug "Red triangle slug") (*Triboniophorus graeffei*) and pink slug (*[Triboniophorus sp. nov. 'Kaputar'](/wiki/Triboniophorus_sp._nov._%27Kaputar%27 "Triboniophorus sp. nov. 'Kaputar'")*), which are known to appear after rainfall.[[3]](#cite_note-park-id-3)

## History

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Mount_Kaputar_National_Park&action=edit&section=1 "Edit section: History")]

Before it was a national park, the area was used largely as [grazing land](/wiki/Grazing "Grazing") for [domestic animals](/wiki/Domestication "Domestication"). The conditions in the park are very harsh, but several pioneering families lived there, and remnants of their occupation remain. [Sheep](/wiki/Sheep "Sheep") and [cattle](/wiki/Cattle "Cattle") continued to graze on the [plateau](/wiki/Plateau "Plateau") until around the 1950s. It was an isolated place, and the stockmen in charge of the cattle often would not see another human for months at a time.[[2]](#cite_note-park-hist-2)

In 1925, some 775 ha (3 sq mi) of land around Mount Kaputar were declared a "Reserve for Public Recreation". Two years later, a trust, known as the Mount Kaputar Trust, was formed to give guidance on managing the park. The area was expanded to 14,244 ha (55 sq mi) and proclaimed a full national park in 1959. Eight years later, in 1967, the Fund relinquished the duties of controlling the park to the newly established [National Parks and Wildlife Service](/wiki/National_Parks_and_Wildlife_Service_(New_South_Wales) "National Parks and Wildlife Service (New South Wales)"), and the park is still administered by a regional advisory board.[[2]](#cite_note-park-hist-2)

## Facilities and activities

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Mount_Kaputar_National_Park&action=edit&section=2 "Edit section: Facilities and activities")]

In 1965, two cabins were constructed at Dawsons Spring, providing accommodations including a permanent water supply for showers and toilets, and a picnic facility. Today there are three cabins, including one facilitated from Bark Hut.[[2]](#cite_note-park-hist-2)

The park is popular with [rockclimbers](/wiki/Rockclimber "Rockclimber"), and there are 11 walks in the park,[[4]](#cite_note-tourism-info-4) as well as a camping ground. However, the most popular site in the park is Scutts Hut, located upward of [Kurrawonga Falls](/wiki/Kurrawonga_Falls "Kurrawonga Falls").[[5]](#cite_note-park-cult-5) The hut is the former home of the Scutt family, a [pioneer family](/wiki/Settler "Settler") living in the vicinity of the park.[[2]](#cite_note-park-hist-2) it is accessible via a fire trail from the Bark Hut camping grounds. The hut has been restored to its original livery, with an earthen floor and an open fireplace. The hut is built on the banks of Horsearm Creek, which flows into Kurrawonga Falls.[[5]](#cite_note-park-cult-5)

* [![Euglah Rock](//upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/thumb/0/07/Eugah_Rock_02.jpg/120px-Eugah_Rock_02.jpg)](/wiki/File:Eugah_Rock_02.jpg "Euglah Rock")
